title: Wet-transition specialist model
summary: Wet and intermediate tyre usage changes race strategy and can remove the normal dry-compound obligation; F1 sources note drivers may be called in when grip conditions change.
rationale: Wet and intermediate tyre usage changes race strategy and can remove the normal dry-compound obligation; F1 sources note drivers may be called in when grip conditions change. Current features mark wet tyres, but may miss transition moments where lap times spike and the field switches compounds.
implementation_hint: Train a small specialist or add features for weather-transition regimes: current/previous lap wet/inter share, slick-to-wet and wet-to-slick wave indicators, field median LapTime_Delta spike, lap-time dispersion spike, first wet compound seen in race, and interactions with PitStop debt and mandatory dry-compound features. Blend specialist predictions only when any wet/inter evidence exists.
expected_effect: Likely improves rare but high-signal wet-condition rows that tree models may underweight in a global model; could also reduce false pressure from dry-compound rules during wet races.
risk: Wet rows may be sparse, so a specialist can overfit. Apply only as features or low-weight blend unless CV confirms gain on wet-heavy folds.
